rationale: Builds directly on the Late Projection EMA Finalizer instead of restarting. The new change keeps the same late projection EMA path, but stops applying EMA wholesale at save time: for each still-int8 projection matrix row, it compares the live row and EMA row under the exact final roundtrip proxy and keeps whichever row is more quantization-stable before the final snap. That compounds the winning EMA idea while avoiding rows where EMA over-smooths useful late updates, keeps artifact size effectively unchanged, and trims a bit of helper code to stay lean under the 1500-line cap.
title: Selective Projection EMA Row Finalizer
